title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: vignette documents probability distributions exported DPmixGPD corresponding d/p/q/r functions used throughout package. continuous random variable YY parameter scalars 𝛉\boldsymbol{\theta}, use standard quartet: Density (PDF): f(y∣𝛉)f(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}), implemented d*(). Distribution (CDF): F(y∣𝛉)=Pr(Y≤y)F(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta})=\Pr(Y\le y), implemented p*(). Quantile: Q(p∣𝛉)=inf{y:F(y∣𝛉)≥p}Q(p\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}) = \inf\{y: F(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta})\ge p\}, implemented q*(). Random generation: Y=Q(U∣𝛉)Y = Q(U\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}) U∼Unif(0,1)U\sim \text{Unif}(0,1), implemented r*(). d*() functions optionally return log-densities via log = TRUE, .e., logf(y∣𝛉)\log f(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}). numerically convenient likelihoods multiply densities add log-densities: log{∏=1nf(yi∣𝛉)}=∑=1nlogf(yi∣𝛉). \log\Big\{\prod_{=1}^n f(y_i\mid\boldsymbol{\theta})\Big\}=\sum_{=1}^n \log f(y_i\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}). p*() functions support lower.tail log.p: Pr(Y>y)=1−F(y),logF(y)orlog{1−F(y)}. \Pr(Y>y)=1-F(y),\qquad \log F(y)\ \text{}\ \log\{1-F(y)\}.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Several kernels available finite mixtures. Let fj(⋅∣𝛉j)f_j(\cdot\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j) denote jjth component density let 𝐰=(w1,…,wJ)\boldsymbol{w}=(w_1,\ldots,w_J) mixture weights wj≥0w_j\ge 0 ∑j=1Jwj=1\sum_{j=1}^J w_j=1. mixture density CDF f(y)=∑j=1Jwjfj(y∣𝛉j),F(y)=∑j=1JwjFj(y∣𝛉j). f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j f_j(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j), \qquad F(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j F_j(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j). Random generation proceeds sampling latent component index Z∼Categorical(𝐰)Z\sim\text{Categorical}(\boldsymbol{w}), sampling Y∣Z=j∼fj(⋅∣𝛉j)Y\mid Z=j\sim f_j(\cdot\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j). Mixture quantiles typically require numerical inversion mixture CDF.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) used tail modeling relative threshold uu. spliced kernels (*Gpd functions), bulk kernel used uu GPD tail attached uu, parameters (u,σ,ξ)(u,\sigma,\xi). code, represented threshold = u, tail_scale = \sigma, tail_shape = \xi (standalone GPD functions use scale shape σ\sigma ξ\xi).

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: standalone GPD functions dGpd, pGpd, qGpd, rGpd implement distribution XXabove threshold uu. x≥ux\ge u, density f(x;u,σ,ξ)=1σ(1+ξx−uσ)−1/ξ−1,σ>0,1+ξx−uσ>0. f(x;u,\sigma,\xi)=\frac{1}{\sigma}\left(1+\xi\frac{x-u}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\xi-1}, \quad \sigma>0, \quad 1+\xi\frac{x-u}{\sigma}>0. CDF F(x)=1−(1+ξx−uσ)−1/ξ,x≥u, F(x)=1-\left(1+\xi\frac{x-u}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\xi}, \quad x\ge u,  quantile Q(p)=u+σξ[(1−p)−ξ−1],p∈(0,1),ξ≠0, Q(p)=u+\frac{\sigma}{\xi}\Big[(1-p)^{-\xi}-1\Big],\quad p\(0,1),\ \xi\ne 0,  exponential-limit case obtained ξ→0\xi\0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\toscale, ξ→\xi\toshape. Log-densities returned log = TRUE. show DPmixGPD kernels pairs: bulk-GPD-augmented. GPD tail threshold uu uses fGPD(x;u,σ,ξ)=1σ(1+ξx−uσ)−1/ξ−1for x≥u.f_{GPD}(x; u, \sigma, \xi) = \frac{1}{\sigma} \left(1 + \xi \frac{x-u}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\xi - 1} \ \text{} x \ge u. standalone GPD tail distribution available via dGpd, pGpd, qGpd, rGpd functions.  subsection defines available d/p/q/rd/p/q/r functions, prints example outputs fixed parameters, overlays density curves three parameter sets clear legends. parameter sets reused within bulk GPD variants section.
code: dGpd(1.8, threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2) [1] 1.013262 dGpd(1.8, threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2, log = TRUE) [1] 0.01317507 pGpd(1.8, threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2) [1] 0.4325731 pGpd(1.8, threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2, lower.tail = FALSE) [1] 0.5674269 pGpd(1.8, threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2, log.p = TRUE) [1] -0.8380038 q_vec(qGpd, c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2) [1] 1.648060 1.871746 2.298770 q_vec(qGpd, c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2,       lower.tail = FALSE) [1] 2.298770 1.871746 1.648060 q_vec(qGpd, c(log(0.25), log(0.5), log(0.75)), threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5,       shape = 0.2, log.p = TRUE) [1] 1.648060 1.871746 2.298770 draw_many(rGpd, list(threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2)) [1] 3.091271 3.346863 1.674344 2.565162 2.069741 grid <- seq(-4, 15, length.out = 500) gpd_sets <- list(   list(label = "GPD A", threshold = 1.5, tail_scale = 0.5, tail_shape = 0.2),   list(label = "GPD B", threshold = 2.0, tail_scale = 0.6, tail_shape = 0.15),   list(label = "GPD C", threshold = 1.0, tail_scale = 0.4, tail_shape = 0.25) )  df_gpd <- do.call(rbind, lapply(gpd_sets, function(ps) {   data.frame(x = grid, density = density_curve(grid, dGpd, list(threshold = ps$threshold, scale = ps$tail_scale, shape = ps$tail_shape)), label = ps$label) }))  ggplot(df_gpd, aes(x = x, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Standalone GPD tails", x = "x", y = "density") +   theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "top")

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: normal component parameterized μ∈ℝ\mu\\mathbb{R} σ>0\sigma>0: f(y∣μ,σ)=12πσexp(−(y−μ)22σ2). f(y\mid\mu,\sigma)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}\sigma}\exp\!\left(-\frac{(y-\mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right). finite normal mixture JJ components density f(y)=∑j=1Jwj𝒩(y∣μj,σj2),∑j=1Jwj=1. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\mathcal{N}(y\mid\mu_j,\sigma_j^2), \qquad \sum_{j=1}^J w_j=1. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μj→\mu_j\tomean[j], σj→\sigma_j\tosd[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: spliced version, bulk distribution normal mixture uu, GPD tail attached uu. tail parameters σ>0\sigma>0 ξ\xi, applied exceedances x−ux-u. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
code: grid <- seq(-4, 5, length.out = 400) normal_sets <- list(   list(label = "Mix A", w = c(0.6, 0.3, 0.1), mean = c(-1, 0.5, 2), sd = c(2, 0.6, 1.1)),   list(label = "Mix B", w = c(0.5, 0.3, 0.2), mean = c(-1.2, 0.3, 1.5), sd = c(0.9, 0.7, 1.0)),   list(label = "Mix C", w = c(0.4, 0.35, 0.25), mean = c(-0.5, 2, 2.5), sd = c(0.7, 0.6, 1.2)) )  example <- normal_sets[[1]] dNormMix(0, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd) [1] 0.2535206 dNormMix(0, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, log = TRUE) [1] -1.37231 pNormMix(0, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd) [1] 0.4790278 pNormMix(0, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, lower.tail = FALSE) [1] 0.5209722 pNormMix(0, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, log.p = TRUE) [1] -0.7359966 q_vec(qNormMix, c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), w = example$w, mean = example$mean,       sd = example$sd) [1] -1.42337727  0.08046623  0.94945934 q_vec(qNormMix, c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), w = example$w, mean = example$mean,       sd = example$sd, lower.tail = FALSE) [1]  0.94945934  0.08046623 -1.42337727 q_vec(qNormMix, c(log(0.25), log(0.5), log(0.75)), w = example$w,       mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, log.p = TRUE) [1] -1.42337727  0.08046623  0.94945934 draw_many(rNormMix, list(w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd)) [1]  0.2657252  0.8234143  1.4069132  2.1097911 -1.1254282 df_norm <- do.call(rbind, lapply(normal_sets, function(ps) {   data.frame(x = grid, density = density_curve(grid, dNormMix, list(w = ps$w, mean = ps$mean, sd = ps$sd)), label = ps$label) }))  ggplot(df_norm, aes(x = x, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Normal mixtures (bulk)", x = "x", y = "density") +   theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "top")

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Spliced density uses mixture x<ux<u attaches fGPDf_{GPD} uu continuity. CDF combines mixture CDF uu GPD exceedance beyond uu; quantiles invert spliced CDF; RNG draws bulk vs tail CDF mass uu.
code: normal_gpd_sets <- list(   list(label = "Mix A", w = c(0.6, 0.4), mean = c(-1, 2), sd = c(0.5, 0.8), threshold = 1.8, tail_scale = 0.4, tail_shape = 0.25),   list(label = "Mix B", w = c(0.5, 0.5), mean = c(0, 1), sd = c(0.6, 0.6), threshold = 1.5, tail_scale = 0.3, tail_shape = 0.2),   list(label = "Mix C", w = c(0.7, 0.3), mean = c(0.5, 2.5), sd = c(0.4, 1.0), threshold = 2.0, tail_scale = 0.5, tail_shape = 0.15) )  example <- normal_gpd_sets[[1]] dNormMixGpd(2, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, threshold = example$threshold, tail_scale = example$tail_scale, tail_shape = example$tail_shape) [1] 0.3322395 pNormMixGpd(2, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, threshold = example$threshold, tail_scale = example$tail_scale, tail_shape = example$tail_shape) [1] 0.8504922 q_vec(qNormMixGpd, c(0.5, 0.9), w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, threshold = example$threshold, tail_scale = example$tail_scale, tail_shape = example$tail_shape) [1] -0.5173894  2.1903912 draw_many(rNormMixGpd, example) [1]  1.8608586  3.5294576 -1.1393944  2.5087603 -0.1251643 df_norm_gpd <- do.call(rbind, lapply(normal_gpd_sets, function(ps) {   data.frame(x = grid, density = density_curve(grid, dNormMixGpd, list(w = ps$w, mean = ps$mean, sd = ps$sd, threshold = ps$threshold, tail_scale = ps$tail_scale, tail_shape = ps$tail_shape)), label = ps$label) }))  ggplot(df_norm_gpd, aes(x = x, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Normal mixtures with GPD tail (different thresholds)", x = "x", y = "density") +   theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "top")

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: gamma component shape α>0\alpha>0 scale β>0\beta>0 density f(y∣α,β)=1Γ(α)βαyα−1exp(−yβ),y>0. f(y\mid \alpha,\beta) = \frac{1}{\Gamma(\alpha)\,\beta^\alpha}\,y^{\alpha-1}\exp\!\left(-\frac{y}{\beta}\right), \qquad y>0. finite gamma mixture JJ components f(y)=∑j=1Jwjfj(y∣αj,βj),wj≥0,∑j=1Jwj=1. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,f_j(y\mid \alpha_j,\beta_j), \qquad w_j\ge 0,\ \sum_{j=1}^J w_j=1. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): α→\alpha\toshape, β→\beta\toscale, weights wj→w_j\tow[j]. *MixGpd variant uses splicing idea: gamma mixture uu GPD tail uu. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
code: grid <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 400) gamma_sets <- list(   list(label = "Mix A", w = c(0.6, 0.3, 0.1), shape = c(2.0, 5.0, 9.0), scale = c(1.0, 0.6, 0.3)),   list(label = "Mix B", w = c(0.5, 0.3, 0.2), shape = c(1.5, 4.0, 7.0), scale = c(1.2, 0.7, 0.35)),   list(label = "Mix C", w = c(0.4, 0.3, 0.3), shape = c(1.2, 3.5, 6.0), scale = c(1.4, 0.75, 0.4)) ) example <- gamma_sets[[1]]  dens <- do.call(rbind, lapply(gamma_sets, function(s) {   data.frame(     x = grid,     density = dGammaMix(grid, w = s$w, shape = s$shape, scale = s$scale),     label = s$label   ) })) dGammaMix(2, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale) [1] 0.2952082 dGammaMix(2, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale, log = TRUE) [1] -1.220074 pGammaMix(2, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale) [1] 0.452308 pGammaMix(2, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale, lower.tail = FALSE) [1] 0.547692 pGammaMix(2, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale, log.p = TRUE) [1] -0.7933919 qGammaMix(0.95, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale) [1] 5.008484 qGammaMix(0.95, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale, lower.tail = FALSE) [1] 0.4747765 df_gamma <- do.call(rbind, lapply(gamma_sets, function(ps) {   data.frame(x = grid, density = density_curve(grid, dGammaMix, list(w = ps$w, shape = ps$shape, scale = ps$scale)), label = ps$label) }))  ggplot(df_norm, aes(x = x, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Gamma mixtures (bulk)", x = "x", y = "density") +   theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "top")

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: ## Lognormal

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: lognormal component defined logY∼𝒩(μ,σ2)\log Y \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu,\sigma^2), .e., f(y∣μ,σ)=1yσ2πexp(−(logy−μ)22σ2),y>0. f(y\mid\mu,\sigma)=\frac{1}{y\,\sigma\sqrt{2\pi}}\exp\!\left(-\frac{(\log y-\mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right),\quad y>0. finite lognormal mixture density f(y)=∑j=1JwjLognormal(y∣μj,σj2),y>0. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\text{Lognormal}(y\mid\mu_j,\sigma_j^2),\quad y>0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μj→\mu_j\tomeanlog[j], σj→\sigma_j\tosdlog[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: *MixGpd variant uses splicing idea: lognormal mixture uu, GPD tail uu. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Laplace (double-exponential) component location ℓ∈ℝ\ell\\mathbb{R} scale b>0b>0 density f(y∣ℓ,b)=12bexp(−|y−ℓ|b),y∈ℝ. f(y\mid \ell,b)=\frac{1}{2b}\exp\!\left(-\frac{|y-\ell|}{b}\right),\quad y\\mathbb{R}. finite Laplace mixture f(y)=∑j=1JwjLaplace(y∣ℓj,bj). f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\text{Laplace}(y\mid \ell_j,b_j). Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): ℓj→\ell_j\tolocation[j], bj→b_j\toscale[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: inverse Gaussian component mean μ>0\mu>0 shape λ>0\lambda>0 density f(y∣μ,λ)=(λ2πy3)1/2exp(−λ(y−μ)22μ2y),y>0. f(y\mid \mu,\lambda) = \left(\frac{\lambda}{2\pi y^3}\right)^{1/2} \exp\!\left(-\frac{\lambda (y-\mu)^2}{2\mu^2 y}\right), \quad y>0. finite inverse Gaussian mixture f(y)=∑j=1JwjIG(y∣μj,λj),y>0. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\text{IG}(y\mid \mu_j,\lambda_j),\quad y>0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μj→\mu_j\tomean[j], λj→\lambda_j\toshape[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Amoroso component location ∈ℝa\\mathbb{R}, scale θ≠0\theta\neq 0, shapes α>0\alpha>0, β>0\beta>0 density (y>ay>) f(y∣,θ,α,β)=1Γ(α)|βθ|(y−aθ)αβ−1exp[−(y−aθ)β]. f(y\mid ,\theta,\alpha,\beta) = \frac{1}{\Gamma(\alpha)} \left|\frac{\beta}{\theta}\right| \left(\frac{y-}{\theta}\right)^{\alpha\beta-1} \exp\!\left[-\left(\frac{y-}{\theta}\right)^{\beta}\right]. finite Amoroso mixture f(y)=∑j=1Jwjfj(y∣aj,θj,αj,βj). f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,f_j(y\mid a_j,\theta_j,\alpha_j,\beta_j). Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): aj→a_j\toloc[j], θj→\theta_j\toscale[j], αj→\alpha_j\toshape1[j], βj→\beta_j\toshape2[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: section documents inverse Gaussian density , single component rather mixture: f(y∣μ,λ)=(λ2πy3)1/2exp(−λ(y−μ)22μ2y),y>0. f(y\mid \mu,\lambda) = \left(\frac{\lambda}{2\pi y^3}\right)^{1/2} \exp\!\left(-\frac{\lambda (y-\mu)^2}{2\mu^2 y}\right), \quad y>0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μ→\mu\tomean, λ→\lambda\toshape.

what: Inverse Gaussian base with GPD tail
title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: dInvGaussGpd, pInvGaussGpd, qInvGaussGpd, rInvGaussGpd functions splice base inverse Gaussian uu GPD tail uu. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Cauchy distribution location x0∈ℝx_0\\mathbb{R} scale γ>0\gamma>0 density f(y∣x0,γ)=1πγ[1+(y−x0γ)2],y∈ℝ. f(y\mid x_0,\gamma)=\frac{1}{\pi\gamma\left[1+\left(\frac{y-x_0}{\gamma}\right)^2\right]}, \quad y\\mathbb{R}. finite Cauchy mixture f(y)=∑j=1JwjCauchy(y∣x0j,γj). f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\text{Cauchy}(y\mid x_{0j},\gamma_j). Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): x0→x_0\tolocation, γ→\gamma\toscale; mixtures x0j→x_{0j}\tolocation[j], γj→\gamma_j\toscale[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: section provides compact reference distributions supported DPmixGPD corresponding user-facing function families. CRP backend uses base (single-kernel) functions, SB backend uses mixture (multi-component) functions. “Type” column indicates whether backend expects scalar parameters (single value per parameter) vectors indexed mixture component.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: CRP Backend uses base (single-kernel) functions; parameters scalar. SB Backend uses mixture/spliced mixture functions; component-specific parameters typically vectors indexed component jj; mixture weights w required vector. GPD-, SB entries NA mixture-GPD wrapper SB family.

title: 3. Basic Workflow: Model Specification, Bundle, & MCMC
text: DPmixGPD uses three-phase model-building paradigm designed clarity control: Specification (compile_model_spec): Validate inputs, NIMBLE code generation Bundle (build_nimble_bundle): Generate NIMBLE code, compile sampler MCMC (run_mcmc_bundle_manual): Execute posterior sampling

title: 3. Basic Workflow: Model Specification, Bundle, & MCMC
text: Purpose: Validate data, kernels, backends, parameters WITHOUT generating NIMBLE code.
code:
# Load packaged data data("nc_pos200_k3") y <- nc_pos200_k3$y  # Create specification spec <- compile_model_spec(   y = y,   kernel = "gamma",   backend = "crp",   GPD = FALSE )  # Inspect specification print("Specification object class:", class(spec), "
") print("Kernel chosen:", spec$kernel, "
") print("Backend chosen:", spec$backend, "
") print("GPD enabled:", spec$GPD, "
") print("Sample size n:", length(spec$y), "
")

title: 3. Basic Workflow: Model Specification, Bundle, & MCMC
code: # 1. Unconditional DPmix (bulk only, no tail) spec_unconditional <- compile_model_spec(y, kernel = "gamma", backend = "crp", GPD = FALSE)  # 2. Unconditional DPmixGPD (bulk + tail) spec_gpd <- compile_model_spec(y, kernel = "gamma", backend = "crp", GPD = TRUE)  # 3. Conditional (with covariates) data("nc_posX100_p3_k2") y_cond <- nc_posX100_p3_k2$y X <- as.matrix(nc_posX100_p3_k2$X) spec_conditional <- compile_model_spec(   y = y_cond,   X = X,   kernel = "lognormal",   backend = "crp",   GPD = FALSE )  # 4. With custom MCMC settings spec_custom <- compile_model_spec(   y = y,   kernel = "gamma",   backend = "crp",   GPD = FALSE,   mcmc = list(niter = 1500, nburnin = 250, nchains = 2, thin = 2) )  print("Created 4 specification objects successfully.\n")

title: 4. Unconditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: Goal: Estimate density univariate outcome yy using nonparametric Dirichlet Process mixture Chinese Restaurant Process backend. Model: yi|G∼∫K(yi;θ)dG(θ)y_i | G \sim \int K(y_i; \theta) dG(\theta) G∼DP(α,G0)G \sim \text{DP}(\alpha, G_0) Backend: CRP truncation max components

title: 4. Unconditional DPmix with CRP Backend
code: # Load pre-generated dataset: 200 observations from mixture of 3 gamma components data(nc_pos200_k3) y_mixed <- nc_pos200_k3$y  paste("Sample size:", length(y_mixed)) [1] "Sample size: 200" paste("Mean:", mean(y_mixed)) [1] "Mean: 4.21476750434594" paste("SD:", sd(y_mixed)) [1] "SD: 4.10835046697183" paste("Range:", paste(range(y_mixed), collapse = " to ")) [1] "Range: 0.0403111680208858 to 19.6013451514889"  # Visualization df_data <- data.frame(y = y_mixed) p_raw <- ggplot(df_data, aes(x = y)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30, alpha = 0.6, fill = "steelblue",color = "black") +   geom_density(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Raw Data: Mixed Gamma Distribution", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal()  print(p_raw)

title: 4. Unconditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: ’ll use build_nimble_bundle function directly handles specification bundle creation.
code: bundle_crp <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y_mixed,   kernel = "laplace",         # Use laplace kernel   backend = "crp",            # CRP backend   GPD = FALSE,                # No tail augmentation   components = 3,             # Minimal for testing   alpha_random = TRUE,        # Random DP concentration   mcmc = list(     niter = 50,             # Minimal for testing     nburnin = 10,           # Minimal burnin     nchains = 2,            # Two chains for diagnostics     thin = 1                # No thinning   ) )

title: 4. Unconditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: CRP Backend: Flexible component allocation, ideal unknown mixture complexity components Parameter: Higher components allows components increases computation Kernel Choice: Gamma suitable positive, skewed data Diagnostics: Check convergence (Rhat, ESS) posterior predictive fit Next: Compare Stick-Breaking (SB) backend vignette 5

title: 5. Unconditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Purpose: Showcase stick-breaking backend uses fixed number mixture components (components = J) contrast CRP backend v04. vignette fit two bulk kernels (Gamma Cauchy) dataset highlight heavier tails bulk can change fit even without GPD tail.

title: 5. Unconditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Summary SB Dataset
code: # Load benchmark dataset data("nc_pos200_k3") y_mixed <- nc_pos200_k3$y  df_data <- data.frame(y = y_mixed)  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y_mixed), mean(y_mixed), sd(y_mixed), min(y_mixed), max(y_mixed)) )  p_raw <- ggplot(df_data, aes(x = y)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30, fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.7, color = "black") +   geom_density(color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.2) +   labs(title = "Raw Data: Mixed Gamma Distribution", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal()  grid.arrange(p_raw, ncol = 1)

title: 5. Unconditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: build stick-breaking mixture explicitly components = 5 room weight decay keeping MCMC runtime manageable vignette. refit SB model Cauchy kernel comparison.
code: # --- Gamma kernel --- bundle_sb_gamma <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y_mixed,   kernel = "gamma",   backend = "sb",   components = 5,            # fixed truncation level for SB   GPD = FALSE,               # bulk-only scenario   mcmc = list(     niter = 60,     nburnin = 10,     nchains = 2,     thin = 1   ) )  # --- Cauchy kernel --- bundle_sb_cauchy <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y_mixed,   kernel = "cauchy",   backend = "sb",   components = 5,   GPD = FALSE,   mcmc = list(     niter = 60,     nburnin = 10,     nchains = 1,     thin = 1   ) )

title: 5. Unconditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
code: y_grid <- seq(min(y_mixed), max(y_mixed) * 1.3, length.out = 250)  pred_density_gamma <- predict(fit_sb_gamma, y = y_grid, type = "density") pred_density_cauchy <- predict(fit_sb_cauchy, y = y_grid, type = "density")  plot(pred_density_gamma) plot(pred_density_cauchy) quant_probs <- c(0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95)  pred_q_gamma <- predict(fit_sb_gamma, type = "quantile", index = quant_probs, interval = "credible") pred_q_cauchy <- predict(fit_sb_cauchy, type = "quantile", index = quant_probs, interval = "credible")  plot(pred_q_gamma) plot(pred_q_cauchy) pred_mean_gamma <- predict(fit_sb_gamma, type = "mean") pred_mean_cauchy <- predict(fit_sb_cauchy, type = "mean")  plot(pred_mean_gamma) plot(pred_mean_cauchy)

title: 5. Unconditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: SB Backend: Fixed components keeps label-switching manageable, diagnostic plots via S3 plot() method show weight dynamics. Kernels matter: Gamma vs Cauchy can behave differently shoulders/tails, even without GPD tail. Predictions: Posterior density, quantiles, mean accessible via predict() display credible bands summary. Backend comparison: CRP fit (v04) SB-gamma fit deliver similar central quantiles SB offers control truncation. Next: Explore tail augmentation (GPD = TRUE) CRP backend v06 SB backend v07.

what: Unconditional DPmixGPD: CRP Backend with Tail Augmentation
title: 6. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
text: Purpose: Model bulk distribution DP mixture (“kernel” bulk family) let GPD tail capture extreme values beyond threshold. CRP backend handles partitioning toggle GPD = TRUE augment tail.

previous_headings: Unconditional DPmixGPD: CRP Backend with Tail Augmentation
title: 6. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
text: Summary Tail Dataset
code: # Load data with an obvious tail data("nc_pos_tail200_k4") y_tail <- nc_pos_tail200_k4$y y_tail <- y_tail[is.finite(y_tail) & y_tail > 0]  # Summaries and table summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y_tail), mean(y_tail), sd(y_tail), min(y_tail), max(y_tail)) )  df_data <- data.frame(y = y_tail)  ggplot(df_data, aes(x = y)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40, fill = "steelblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.6) +   geom_density(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Raw Data: Mix of Bulk and Tail", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal()

previous_headings: Unconditional DPmixGPD: CRP Backend with Tail Augmentation
title: 6. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
code: thresholds <- quantile(y_tail, c(0.70, 0.75, 0.80, 0.85, 0.90)) u_threshold <- thresholds["80%"]  df_tail <- tibble(   y = y_tail,   region = ifelse(y_tail > u_threshold, "Tail", "Bulk") )  ggplot(df_tail, aes(x = y, fill = region)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40, alpha = 0.7, color = "black") +   geom_vline(xintercept = u_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Bulk" = "steelblue", "Tail" = "red")) +   labs(title = "Threshold Partition", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal()

previous_headings: Unconditional DPmixGPD: CRP Backend with Tail Augmentation
title: 6. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
text: mirrors direct build_nimble_bundle() workflow v04, tail augmentation turned (GPD = TRUE). use Inverse Gaussian bulk kernel estimate GPD threshold lognormal prior centered empirical 80th percentile.
code: bundle_gpd <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y_tail,   kernel = "invgauss",   backend = "crp",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 5,   alpha_random = TRUE,   param_specs = list(     gpd = list(       threshold = list(         mode = "dist",         dist = "lognormal",         args = list(meanlog = log(max(u_threshold, .Machine$double.eps)), sdlog = 0.25)       )     )   ),   mcmc = list(     niter = 60,     nburnin = 10,     nchains = 2,     thin = 1   ) )

Processing results...  sensitivity_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(sensitivity_fits, function(entry) {   quant <- predict(entry$fit, type = "quantile", index = 0.99, interval = "credible")   qfit <- as.data.frame(quant$fit)   qfit$threshold <- entry$threshold    # Keep the usual columns if present   keep <- intersect(names(qfit), c("threshold", "index", "estimate", "lower", "upper", "lwr", "upr"))   qfit <- qfit[, keep, drop = FALSE]    # Standardize to one set of CI column names   if (!"lower" %in% names(qfit) && "lwr" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$lower <- qfit$lwr   if (!"upper" %in% names(qfit) && "upr" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$upper <- qfit$upr    # If estimate isn't present for some reason, fall back to the first numeric column   if (!"estimate" %in% names(qfit)) {     num_cols <- names(qfit)[vapply(qfit, is.numeric, logical(1))]     if (length(num_cols) > 0) qfit$estimate <- qfit[[num_cols[1]]]   }    out <- data.frame(     threshold = qfit$threshold[1],     q_99 = qfit$estimate[1],     q_99_lwr = if ("lower" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$lower[1] else NA_real_,     q_99_upr = if ("upper" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$upper[1] else NA_real_   )   tibble::as_tibble(out) }))  sensitivity_tbl %>%   mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>%   kable(caption = "Threshold Sensitivity: 99th Quantile", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center")

title: 6. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
text: Bulk + Tail: DPmix explains bulk, GPD captures extremes. Threshold: 0.8 quantile running default, sensitivity checks guide choice. Next: Explore Stick-Breaking (v07) another backend tail augmentation.

what: Unconditional DPmixGPD: Stick-Breaking (SB) Backend with Tail Augmentation
title: 7. Unconditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Purpose: Demonstrate stick-breaking backend (components = J) augmenting extreme tail GPD. mirrors CRP+GPD pipeline (v06) highlights fixed-truncation behavior bulk components.

previous_headings: Unconditional DPmixGPD: Stick-Breaking (SB) Backend with Tail Augmentation
title: 7. Unconditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Tail Dataset Summaries
code: # Tail-heavy data data("nc_pos_tail200_k4") y_tail <- nc_pos_tail200_k4$y  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y_tail), mean(y_tail), sd(y_tail), min(y_tail), max(y_tail)) )  df_data <- data.frame(y = y_tail)  p_raw <- ggplot(df_data, aes(x = y)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40, fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.6, color = "black") +   geom_density(color = "darkred", linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Tail-Designed Data", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal()  grid.arrange(p_raw, ncol = 1)

title: 7. Unconditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
code: thresholds <- quantile(y_tail, c(0.70, 0.75, 0.80, 0.85)) u_threshold <- thresholds["80%"]  ggplot(df_data, aes(x = y)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40, fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.6, color = "black") +   geom_vline(xintercept = u_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +   labs(title = paste("Threshold at", round(u_threshold, 2)), x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal()

title: 7. Unconditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: follows structure SB bulk-vignette (v05): build bundle build_nimble_bundle(), run MCMC, use S3 predict() + plot() helpers. Compared v06 (CRP+GPD), keep stick-breaking backend use Gamma bulk kernel lognormal threshold prior, contrast bulk-Laplace fit.
code: bundle_sb_gpd <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y_tail,   kernel = "gamma",   backend = "sb",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 5,   param_specs = list(     gpd = list(       threshold = list(         mode = "dist",         dist = "lognormal",         args = list(meanlog = log(max(u_threshold, .Machine$double.eps)), sdlog = 0.25)       )     )   ),   mcmc = list(     niter = 60,     nburnin = 10,     nchains = 2,     thin = 1   ) )

text: Stick-breaking truncation fixes number bulk components still flexibly models tail via GPD. Posterior predict() + plot() workflows visualize densities, survival probabilities, extreme quantiles. what: Conditional DPmix: CRP Backend with Covariates
title: 8. Conditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: Purpose: Show CRP backend can model y|Xy | X via covariate-dependent Dirichlet Process mixture. vignette parallels v04 includes XX can explore conditional predictions.

previous_headings: Conditional DPmix: CRP Backend with Covariates
title: 8. Conditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: Summary Conditional Dataset
code: data("nc_posX100_p3_k2") y <- nc_posX100_p3_k2$y X <- as.matrix(nc_posX100_p3_k2$X) if (is.null(colnames(X))) {   colnames(X) <- paste0("x", seq_len(ncol(X))) }  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  df_cov <- data.frame(y = y, x1 = X[, 1], x2 = X[, 2])  p_cov <- ggplot(df_cov, aes(x = x1, y = y)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "steelblue") +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "firebrick", fill = NA) +   labs(title = "y vs X1 (loess smoother)", x = "X1", y = "y") +   theme_minimal()  grid.arrange(p_cov, ncol = 1)

title: 8. Conditional DPmix with CRP Backend
code: X_new <- expand.grid(   x1 = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 3),   x2 = c(0, 1),   x3 = 0 ) colnames(X_new) <- colnames(X)  y_grid <- seq(-1, 10, length.out = 200) densities_normal <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_cond_normal, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     group = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Normal"   ) })  densities_amoroso <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_cond_amoroso, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     group = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Amoroso (shape1=1)"   ) })  df_dens <- bind_rows(densities_normal, densities_amoroso)  ggplot(df_dens, aes(x = y, y = density, color = group)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "Conditional Predictive Densities", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: 8. Conditional DPmix with CRP Backend
code: X_grid <- cbind(   x1 = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 5),   x2 = 0,   x3 = 0 ) colnames(X_grid) <- colnames(X)  quant_probs <- c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75) pred_q_normal <- predict(fit_cond_normal, x = as.matrix(X_grid), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs) pred_q_amoroso <- predict(fit_cond_amoroso, x = as.matrix(X_grid), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs)  quant_df_normal <- pred_q_normal$fit quant_df_normal$x1 <- X_grid[quant_df_normal$id, "x1"] quant_df_normal$model <- "Normal"  quant_df_amoroso <- pred_q_amoroso$fit quant_df_amoroso$x1 <- X_grid[quant_df_amoroso$id, "x1"] quant_df_amoroso$model <- "Amoroso (shape1=1)"  bind_rows(quant_df_normal, quant_df_amoroso) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = x1, y = estimate, color = factor(index), group = index)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "Conditional Quantiles vs x1 (x2=0)", x = "x1", y = "y", color = "Quantile") +   theme_minimal()

title: 8. Conditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: Covariate-informed DP mixtures predict outcome distributions shift x1 (covariates). Use predict(..., type = "density") visualize conditional densities type = "quantile" location shifts. Diagnostics (plot(fit_cond_normal), fitted(fit_cond_normal)) ensure chains mix relying predictions. Next vignette extends idea SB backend adding tails.

title: 9. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Purpose: Replace CRP backend stick-breaking truncation keeping covariate-dependent bulk structure. demonstrates fixed components interplay covariates.

title: 9. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Conditional Dataset Summary (SB)
code: data("nc_posX100_p3_k2") y <- nc_posX100_p3_k2$y X <- as.matrix(nc_posX100_p3_k2$X) if (is.null(colnames(X))) {   colnames(X) <- paste0("x", seq_len(ncol(X))) }  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  ggplot(data.frame(y = y, x1 = X[, 1]), aes(x = x1, y = y)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "darkorange") +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "navy", fill = NA) +   labs(title = "y vs X1 (SB)", x = "X1", y = "y") +   theme_minimal()

title: 9. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
code: X_new <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 3), x2 = c(-1, 1), x3 = 0) colnames(X_new) <- colnames(X) y_min <- max(min(y), .Machine$double.eps) y_grid <- seq(y_min, max(y) * 1.1, length.out = 200)  densities_normal <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_sb_normal, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Normal"   ) })  densities_cauchy <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_sb_cauchy, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Cauchy"   ) })  df_cond <- bind_rows(densities_normal, densities_cauchy)  ggplot(df_cond, aes(x = y, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "SB Conditional Predictive Densities", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: 9. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
code: X_eval <- cbind(x1 = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 5), x2 = 0, x3 = 0) colnames(X_eval) <- colnames(X) quant_probs <- c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)  pred_q_normal <- predict(fit_sb_normal, x = as.matrix(X_eval), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs) pred_q_cauchy <- predict(fit_sb_cauchy, x = as.matrix(X_eval), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs)  quant_df_normal <- pred_q_normal$fit quant_df_normal$x1 <- X_eval[quant_df_normal$id, "x1"] quant_df_normal$model <- "Normal"  quant_df_cauchy <- pred_q_cauchy$fit quant_df_cauchy$x1 <- X_eval[quant_df_cauchy$id, "x1"] quant_df_cauchy$model <- "Cauchy"  bind_rows(quant_df_normal, quant_df_cauchy) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = x1, y = estimate, color = factor(index), group = index)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "SB Conditional Quantiles vs x1", x = "x1", y = "y", color = "Quantile") +   theme_minimal()

title: 9. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Stick-breaking component count fixed still supports covariate-dependent mixtures. predict(..., type = "density") returns group-specific densities X. predict(..., type = "quantile") shows median 75% quantiles shift x1. Diagnoses rely S3 plot()/fitted() pipeline available vignettes.

text: Purpose: Combine conditional modeling GPD tail augmentation covariate slice inherits mixture bulk tail behavior. extends unconditional GPD (v06) conditional DP (v08).

title: 10. Conditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
code: X_new <- rbind(   c(-1, 0, 0, 0, 0),   c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0),   c(1, 1, 0, 0, 0) ) colnames(X_new) <- colnames(X) y_grid <- seq(0, max(y) * 1.2, length.out = 200)  df_pred_lognormal <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_cond_gpd_lognormal, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste("x1=", X_new[i, 1], ", x2=", X_new[i, 2], sep = ""),     model = "Lognormal"   ) })  df_pred_normal <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_cond_gpd_normal, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste("x1=", X_new[i, 1], ", x2=", X_new[i, 2], sep = ""),     model = "Normal"   ) })  bind_rows(df_pred_lognormal, df_pred_normal) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = y, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "Conditional Density with GPD Tail", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

previous_headings: Conditional DPmixGPD: CRP Backend with Tail Augmentation
title: 10. Conditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
code: X_grid <- cbind(x1 = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 5), x2 = 0, x3 = 0, x4 = 0, x5 = 0) colnames(X_grid) <- colnames(X) quant_probs <- c(0.90, 0.95)  pred_q_lognormal <- predict(fit_cond_gpd_lognormal, x = as.matrix(X_grid), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs) pred_q_normal <- predict(fit_cond_gpd_normal, x = as.matrix(X_grid), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs)  quant_df_lognormal <- pred_q_lognormal$fit quant_df_lognormal$x1 <- X_grid[quant_df_lognormal$id, "x1"] quant_df_lognormal$model <- "Lognormal"  quant_df_normal <- pred_q_normal$fit quant_df_normal$x1 <- X_grid[quant_df_normal$id, "x1"] quant_df_normal$model <- "Normal"  bind_rows(quant_df_lognormal, quant_df_normal) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = x1, y = estimate, color = factor(index), group = index)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "Tail Quantiles vs x1 (CRP)", x = "x1", y = "Quantile", color = "Probability") +   theme_minimal()

title: 10. Conditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
text: Conditional DPmix GPD tail lets extreme quantiles vary covariates. CRP backend samples bulk tail jointly thresholding 85th percentile. predict() + plot() remain main tools densities, survival curves, quantiles; residual diagnostics check fit quality. Next: Mirror workflow SB backend v11.

title: 11. Conditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Purpose: Apply fixed-component stick-breaking truncation covariate-dependent mixtures keeping GPD tail. vignette mirrors v10 SB backend.

title: 11. Conditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Conditional Tail Summary (SB)
code: data("nc_posX100_p5_k4") y <- nc_posX100_p5_k4$y X <- as.matrix(nc_posX100_p5_k4$X) if (is.null(colnames(X))) {   colnames(X) <- paste0("x", seq_len(ncol(X))) }  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  ggplot(data.frame(y = y, x1 = X[, 1]), aes(x = x1, y = y)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "purple") +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "orange", fill = NA) +   labs(title = "Tail Outcome vs x1 (SB)", x = "x1", y = "y") +   theme_minimal()

title: 11. Conditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
code: u_threshold <- quantile(y, 0.85)  ggplot(data.frame(y = y), aes(x = y)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40, fill = "lightgreen", alpha = 0.6, color = "black") +   geom_vline(xintercept = u_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +   labs(title = "Threshold for SB tail (85%)", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal()

title: 11. Conditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
code: bundle_sb_cond_gpd_gamma <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   kernel = "gamma",   backend = "sb",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 5,   param_specs = list(     gpd = list(       threshold = list(mode = "link", link = "exp")     )   ),   mcmc = list(     niter = 60,     nburnin = 10,     nchains = 2,     thin = 1   ) )  bundle_sb_cond_gpd_laplace <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   kernel = "laplace",   backend = "sb",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 5,   param_specs = list(     gpd = list(       threshold = list(mode = "link", link = "exp")     )   ),   mcmc = list(     niter = 60,     nburnin = 10,     nchains = 1,     thin = 1   ) )

Expect threshold-selected tail quantiles shift x1 even components fixed. Next: move causal models starting -backend CRP (v12).

title: 12. Causal Inference: Same Backend (CRP) - Normal Kernel
text: vignette fits two CRP-based causal models using kernel (normal): Model : bulk-outcome models (GPD = FALSE) Model B: GPD-augmented outcome models (GPD = TRUE) model demonstrates full causal S3 workflow: bundle, fit, predict, plot, ATE, QTE.

title: 12. Causal Inference: Same Backend (CRP) - Normal Kernel
text: Causal Dataset Summary
code: data("causal_alt_real500_p4_k2") y <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$y T <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$T X <- as.matrix(causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$X)  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  summary_tbl %>%   mutate(value = signif(value, 4)) %>%   kable(caption = "Causal Dataset Summary", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") x_eval <- X[1:40, , drop = FALSE] y_eval <- y[1:40] u_threshold <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(y, 0.8, names = FALSE)) df_causal <- data.frame(y = y, T = as.factor(T), x1 = X[, 1], x2 = X[, 2])  p_scatter <- ggplot(df_causal, aes(x = x1, y = y, color = T)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Outcome vs X1 by Treatment", x = "X1", y = "y", color = "Treatment") +   theme_minimal()  p_scatter

code: data("causal_alt_real500_p4_k2") y <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$y T <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$T X <- as.matrix(causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$X)  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  summary_tbl %>%   mutate(value = signif(value, 4)) %>%   kable(caption = "Causal Dataset Summary", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") x_eval <- X[1:40, , drop = FALSE] y_eval <- y[1:40] u_threshold <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(y, 0.8, names = FALSE)) df_causal <- data.frame(y = y, T = as.factor(T), x1 = X[, 1], x2 = X[, 2])  p_scatter <- ggplot(df_causal, aes(x = x1, y = y, color = T)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Outcome vs X1 by Treatment", x = "X1", y = "y", color = "Treatment") +   theme_minimal()  p_scatter

text: Shifted Outcome Summary (Amoroso)
code: data("causal_alt_real500_p4_k2") y_raw <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$y T <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$T X <- as.matrix(causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$X)  shift <- abs(min(y_raw)) + 0.1 y <- y_raw + shift  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  summary_tbl %>%   mutate(value = signif(value, 4)) %>%   kable(caption = "Shifted Outcome Summary (Amoroso)", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") x_eval <- X[1:40, , drop = FALSE] y_eval <- y[1:40] u_threshold <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(y, 0.8, names = FALSE)) df_causal <- data.frame(y = y, T = as.factor(T), x1 = X[, 1])  p_scatter <- ggplot(df_causal, aes(x = x1, y = y, color = T)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Shifted Outcome vs X1", x = "X1", y = "y (shifted)", color = "Treatment") +   theme_minimal()  p_scatter

code: param_specs_gpd <- list(   gpd = list(     threshold = list(       mode = "dist",       dist = "lognormal",       args = list(meanlog = log(max(u_threshold, .Machine$double.eps)), sdlog = 0.25)     )   ) )  bundle_sb_crp <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   T = T,   X = X,   kernel = c("amoroso", "amoroso"),   backend = c("sb", "crp"),   PS = "logit",   GPD = c(TRUE, TRUE),   J = c(5, 5),   param_specs = param_specs_gpd,   mcmc_outcome = list(niter = 2500, nburnin = 500, nchains = 1, thin = 1, seed = 1),   mcmc_ps = list(niter = 2500, nburnin = 500, nchains = 1, thin = 1, seed = 1) )  bundle_sb_crp DPmixGPD causal bundle PS model: disabled  Outcome (treated): backend = sb | kernel = amoroso  Outcome (control): backend = crp | kernel = amoroso  GPD tail (treated/control): TRUE / TRUE  components (treated/control): 5 / 5  Outcome PS included: FALSE  epsilon (treated/control): 0.025 / 0.025  n (control) = 232 | n (treated) = 268  summary(bundle_sb_crp) DPmixGPD causal bundle summary DPmixGPD causal bundle PS model: disabled  Outcome (treated): backend = sb | kernel = amoroso  Outcome (control): backend = crp | kernel = amoroso  GPD tail (treated/control): TRUE / TRUE  components (treated/control): 5 / 5  Outcome PS included: FALSE  epsilon (treated/control): 0.025 / 0.025  n (control) = 232 | n (treated) = 268 fit_sb_crp <- run_mcmc_causal(bundle_sb_crp) [MCMC] Creating NIMBLE model... 0.055310  0.008794 -0.116000  $tail_shape [1] -0.006654 plot(fit_sb_crp, family = c("traceplot", "autocorrelation", "running"))  === treated ===  === traceplot === === autocorrelation === === running === === control ===  === traceplot === === autocorrelation === === running === pred_mean_sb_crp <- predict(fit_sb_crp, x = x_eval, type = "mean",                             interval = "credible", nsim_mean = 100) plot(pred_mean_sb_crp) pred_q_sb_crp <- predict(fit_sb_crp, x = x_eval, type = "quantile",                          p = 0.5, interval = "credible") plot(pred_q_sb_crp) pred_d_sb_crp <- predict(fit_sb_crp, x = x_eval, y = y_eval,                          type = "density", interval = "credible") plot(pred_d_sb_crp) pred_p_sb_crp <- predict(fit_sb_crp, x = x_eval, y = y_eval,                          type = "prob", interval = "credible") plot(pred_p_sb_crp) ate_sb_crp <- ate(fit_sb_crp, newdata = x_eval,                   interval = "credible", nsim_mean = 100) plot(ate_sb_crp) qte_sb_crp <- qte(fit_sb_crp, probs = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75),                   newdata = x_eval, interval = "credible") plot(qte_sb_crp)

title: Contributing to DPmixGPD
text: Thanks interest contributing! project experimental R package Dirichlet process mixture models optional GPD tails. welcome issues, discussions, pull requests.

title: Contributing to DPmixGPD
text: R >= 4.5.0 (see DESCRIPTION) Windows: Rtools; macOS: Xcode Command Line Tools Suggested: renv dependency management, devtools, roxygen2, testthat Fork clone repository. Open RStudio project (DPmixGPD.Rproj) folder VS Code. Restore deps (using renv): Load package development:
code: install.packages("renv"); renv::restore() install.packages(c("devtools","roxygen2","testthat")) devtools::load_all(quiet = TRUE)

title: Contributing to DPmixGPD
text: Create feature branch master (e.g., feature/-change). Keep PRs focused small. Include tests new behavior update documentation. opening PR: Open PR repo describe motivation, approach, trade-offs.
code: devtools::document()      # update Rd from roxygen2 devtools::test()          # run testthat tests devtools::check()         # run R CMD check

title: Contributing to DPmixGPD
text: Follow tidyverse-style R formatting (use styler desired). Use roxygen2 function documentation; examples runnable guarded. Prefer explicit, vectorized code side effects; avoid premature optimization. Keep changes minimal focused; avoid unrelated refactors.

title: Contributing to DPmixGPD
text: repo includes pre-commit hook runs styler staged .R .Rmd files. enable locally: skip formatting commit, set DPMIXGPD_SKIP_STYLER=1 environment.
code: git config core.hooksPath .githooks

title: Contributing to DPmixGPD
text: Use testthat (edition 3). Place tests tests/testthat/. Aim deterministic tests; mark long-running stochastic tests skip_on_cran() applicable.

title: Contributing to DPmixGPD
text: Search existing issues first. Include reproducible examples (minimal data + code), session info, expected vs actual behavior. File issues : https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD_Package/issues

what: Dirichlet Process Mixtures with Generalized Pareto Tail
title: Dirichlet Process Mixtures with Generalized Pareto Tail
text: DPmixGPD fits Dirichlet process mixture models (CRP stick-breaking backends) optional Generalized Pareto (GPD) tail can model bulk extremes together still get clean, user-friendly prediction APIs.

title: Dirichlet Process Mixtures with Generalized Pareto Tail
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("arnabaich96/DPmixGPD")

title: Dirichlet Process Mixtures with Generalized Pareto Tail
code: library(DPmixGPD)  set.seed(1) y <- abs(rnorm(120)) + 0.2  bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "crp",   kernel  = "lognormal",   GPD     = TRUE,   Kmax    = 6,   mcmc = list(niter = 800, nburnin = 200, thin = 2, nchains = 1, seed = 1) )  fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle)  summary(fit) plot(fit, family = "trace")  # predictive quantities predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9))

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
text: Base Amoroso distribution functions implemented package. Function names parameterization follow existing Amoroso implementation.

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
code: dAmoroso(x, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, log = 0)  pAmoroso(q, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0)  qAmoroso(p, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  rAmoroso(n, loc, scale, shape1, shape2)

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. loc Numeric scalar location parameter. scale Numeric scalar scale parameter. shape1 Numeric scalar first shape parameter. shape2 Numeric scalar second shape parameter. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density (integer flag 0/1 NIMBLE). q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function. n Integer giving number draws. portability inside NIMBLE, RNG implementation supports n = 1.

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
text: Density/CDF/RNG functions return numeric scalars. quantile function returns numeric scalar.

text: dAmoroso(): Density Function Amoroso Distribution pAmoroso(): Distribution Function Amoroso Distribution qAmoroso(): Quantile Function Amoroso Distribution rAmoroso(): Sample generating Function Amoroso Distribution

title: Amoroso mixture distribution — amoroso_mix
text: finite mixture Amoroso components often convenient bulk model response positive right-skewed. functions evaluate simulate \(\sum_j w_j f_{Amoroso}(\cdot\mid loc_j, scale_j, shape1_j, shape2_j)\).

title: Amoroso mixture distribution — amoroso_mix
code: dAmorosoMix(x, w, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, log = 0)  pAmorosoMix(q, w, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0)  rAmorosoMix(n, w, loc, scale, shape1, shape2)  qAmorosoMix(   p,   w,   loc,   scale,   shape1,   shape2,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )

title: Amoroso mixture distribution — amoroso_mix
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. w Numeric vector mixture weights length \(K\). functions treat weights non-negative normalize internally needed. loc Numeric vector length \(K\) giving component locations. scale Numeric vector length \(K\) giving component scales. Negative values flip support corresponding component. shape1 Numeric vector length \(K\) giving first Amoroso shape parameter component. shape2 Numeric vector length \(K\) giving second Amoroso shape parameter component. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density (integer flag 0/1 NIMBLE). q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. portability inside NIMBLE, RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function. tol Numeric scalar tolerance passed stats::uniroot quantile inversion. maxiter Integer maximum number iterations stats::uniroot.

what: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
title: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
text: family splices generalized Pareto distribution (GPD) threshold threshold onto Amoroso mixture bulk. Let \(F_{mix}\) denote Amoroso mixture CDF. spliced CDF \(F(x)=F_{mix}(x)\) \(x<threshold\) \(F(x)=F_{mix}(threshold) + \left\{1-F_{mix}(threshold)\right\}G(x)\) \(x\ge threshold\), \(G\) GPD CDF exceedances threshold.

what: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: Creates causal bundle : propensity score (PS) design model (logit/probit regression T X naive Bayes classifier) outcome bundle control arm (T = 0) outcome bundle treated arm (T = 1)

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
code: build_causal_bundle(   y,   X,   T,   backend = c("sb", "crp"),   kernel,   GPD = FALSE,   components = NULL,   J = NULL,   param_specs = NULL,   mcmc_outcome = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 500, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1),   mcmc_ps = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 500, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1),   epsilon = 0.025,   alpha_random = TRUE,   ps_model = c("logit"),   ps_prior = list(mean = 0, sd = 2),   include_intercept = TRUE,   PS = "logit",   design = c("rct", "observational"),   ps_scale = c("logit", "prob"),   ps_summary = c("mean", "median"),   ps_clamp = 1e-06 )

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: y Numeric outcome vector. X Design matrix data.frame covariates (N x P). T Binary treatment indicator (length N, values 0/1). backend Character; "sb" "crp" outcome models. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). kernel Character kernel name outcome models. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). GPD Logical; include GPD tail outcomes TRUE. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). components Deprecated alias J. one J components supplied. J Integer >= 2; truncation parameter outcome mixtures. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). param_specs Outcome parameter overrides (structure build_nimble_bundle()). can pass single list used arms list con trt entries. mcmc_outcome MCMC settings list outcome bundles. mcmc_ps MCMC settings list PS model. epsilon Numeric [0,1) used outcome bundles posterior truncation summaries. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). alpha_random Logical; whether outcome concentration alpha stochastic. ps_model PS model specification backward compatibility (deprecated). Recommend using PS parameter instead. ps_prior Normal prior PS coefficients. List mean sd. include_intercept Logical; TRUE, intercept column prepended X PS model. PS Character logical; controls propensity score estimation: "logit" (default): Logistic regression PS model "probit": Probit regression PS model "naive": Gaussian naive Bayes PS model FALSE: PS estimation; outcome models use X PS model choice stored bundle metadata downstream use prediction summaries, enabling seamless integration future PS estimation methods. design Causal design: "rct" "observational". ps_scale Scale used augmenting outcomes PS: "logit" "prob". ps_summary Posterior summary PS: "mean" "median". ps_clamp Numeric epsilon clamping PS values \((\epsilon, 1-\epsilon)\).

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) N <- 100 X <- cbind(x1 = rnorm(N), x2 = runif(N)) T <- rbinom(N, 1, plogis(0.3 + 0.5 * X[, 1])) y <- rexp(N) + 0.1  cb <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   T = T,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "gamma",   GPD = TRUE,   J = 10,   PS = "probit" ) } # }

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: Creates runnable "bundle" containing: compiled model spec nimbleCode model code constants, data, explicit dimensions initialization function inits (stored function) monitor specification MCMC settings list (stored used code generation)

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
code: build_nimble_bundle(   y,   X = NULL,   ps = NULL,   backend = c("sb", "crp"),   kernel,   GPD = FALSE,   components = NULL,   J = NULL,   param_specs = NULL,   mcmc = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 500, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1),   epsilon = 0.025,   alpha_random = TRUE )

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: y Numeric outcome vector. X Optional design matrix/data.frame (N x p) conditional variants. ps Numeric vector estimated propensity scores (length N) used covariate constructing link-mode predictors. Mandatory causal inference models (two treatment arms). provided, beta_ps_<param> coefficient vectors added link-mode bulk parameter; priors can customized via param_specs$ps. backend Character; "sb" (stick-breaking) "crp" (Chinese Restaurant Process). kernel Character kernel name (must exist get_kernel_registry()). GPD Logical; whether GPD tail requested. components Deprecated alias J. one J components supplied. J Integer >= 2. Single user-facing truncation parameter: SB: number mixture components used stick-breaking truncation CRP: maximum number clusters represented finite NIMBLE model param_specs Optional list entries bulk, tail, ps override defaults. mcmc Named list MCMC settings (niter, nburnin, thin, nchains, seed). Stored bundle. epsilon Numeric [0,1). downstream summaries/plots/prediction keep smaller k defined either () cumulative mass >= 1 - epsilon (ii) per-component weights >= epsilon, renormalize. alpha_random Logical; whether concentration alpha stochastic.

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: named list (bundle) class "dpmixgpd_bundle".

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: function intentionally stops "pre-run" stage (spec/code/constants/data/dimensions/inits/monitors). Use run_mcmc_bundle_manual() execute MCMC stored settings.

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
code: y <- abs(rnorm(60)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 4,   mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1) ) bundle #> DPmixGPD bundle #>       Field                  Value #>     Backend Stick-Breaking Process #>      Kernel    Normal Distribution #>  Components                      4 #>           N                     60 #>           X                     NO #>         GPD                  FALSE #>     Epsilon                  0.025 #>  #>   contains  : code, constants, data, dimensions, inits, monitors

code: dGammaGpd(x, shape, scale, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape, log = 0)  pGammaGpd(   q,   shape,   scale,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = 1,   log.p = 0 )  rGammaGpd(n, shape, scale, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape)  qGammaGpd(   p,   shape,   scale,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )

title: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. shape Numeric scalar Gamma shape parameter. scale Numeric scalar scale parameter Gamma bulk. threshold Numeric scalar threshold GPD tail attached. tail_scale Numeric scalar GPD scale parameter; must positive. tail_shape Numeric scalar GPD shape parameter. log Integer flag 0/1; 1, return log-density. q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Integer flag 0/1; 1 (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Integer flag 0/1; 1, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function. tol Numeric tolerance numerical inversion qGammaGpd. maxiter Maximum iterations numerical inversion qGammaGpd.

what: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
title: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
text: Splices generalized Pareto distribution (Gpd) threshold onto Lognormal mixture bulk. Let \(F_{mix}\) Lognormal mixture CDF. spliced CDF \(F(x)=F_{mix}(x)\) \(x<threshold\) \(F(x)=F_{mix}(threshold) + \{1-F_{mix}(threshold)\}G(x)\) \(x\ge threshold\), \(G\) Gpd CDF exceedances threshold.

title: Predict from a MixGPD fit — predict.mixgpd_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(stats::rnorm(50)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(y = y, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal",                              GPD = TRUE, components = 6,                              mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1)) fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle) pr <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9)) pr_surv <- predict(fit, y = sort(y), type = "survival") pr_cdf <- list(fit = 1 - pr_surv$fit) } # }

code: library(nimble) #> nimble version 1.4.0 is loaded. #> For more information on NIMBLE and a User Manual, #> please visit https://R-nimble.org. #>  #> Attaching package: 'nimble' #> The following object is masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     simulate #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     declare y <- abs(rnorm(40)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 3,   mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1) ) fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle, show_progress = FALSE) #> Defining model #>   [Note] 'P' is provided in 'constants' but not used in the model code and is being ignored. #> Building model #> Setting data and initial values #> Checking model sizes and dimensions #>   [Note] This model is not fully initialized. This is not an error. #>          To see which variables are not initialized, use model$initializeInfo(). #>          For more information on model initialization, see help(modelInitialization). #> Checking model calculations #> ===== Monitors ===== #> thin = 1: alpha, mean, sd, w, z #> ===== Samplers ===== #> conjugate sampler (6) #>   - mean[]  (3 elements) #>   - sd[]  (3 elements) #> categorical sampler (40) #>   - z[]  (40 elements) #> RW sampler (3) #>   - alpha #>   - v[]  (2 elements) #> Compiling #>   [Note] This may take a minute. #>   [Note] Use 'showCompilerOutput = TRUE' to see C++ compilation details. #> Compiling #>   [Note] This may take a minute. #>   [Note] Use 'showCompilerOutput = TRUE' to see C++ compilation details. #> running chain 1... #>   [Warning] There are 3 individual pWAIC values that are greater than 0.4. This may indicate that the WAIC estimate is unstable (Vehtari et al., 2017), at least in cases without grouping of data nodes or multivariate data nodes. fit #> MixGPD fit | backend: Stick-Breaking Process | kernel: Normal Distribution | GPD tail: FALSE #> n = 40 | components = 3 | epsilon = 0.025 #> MCMC: niter=200, nburnin=50, thin=1, nchains=1  #> Fit #> Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions.
